title: API Design Strategies: GraphQL vs. REST

questions.sentence: Choosing the right API architectural style is a critical decision that impacts frontend development speed and application performance.
For years, REST has been the industry standard due to its simplicity and stateless nature.
However, REST often leads to 'over-fetching' (receiving more data than needed) or 'under-fetching' (needing multiple requests to gather all data).
'GraphQL', a query language for APIs developed by Facebook, addresses these issues by allowing the frontend to request the exact data it needs in a single request.
While GraphQL offers greater flexibility and precision, it introduces complexity on the server side and can be harder to cache than traditional REST endpoints.
